fragility_points: A sustained rally above 75k would weaken the variant case quickly.
The thesis depends more on timing/volatility than on a hidden bearish fundamental.
key_assumptions: A roughly 3% downside move before the April 17 noon ET settlement minute remains plausible for BTC over ~43 hours.
Binance-specific one-minute close mechanics add timing fragility beyond a generic spot-price view.
main_logical_chain: The contract resolves on Binance BTCUSDT's exact April 17 12:00 ET one-minute close, not today's spot price.
BTC is currently above 72k, so Yes is the base case.
But the cushion is only about $2.1k, which BTC can lose within two days under normal volatility.
Therefore Yes remains more likely than not, but 82% is somewhat overconfident for such a narrow settlement rule.
main_thesis: BTC is more likely than not to settle above 72k on April 17, but the 82% market price overstates confidence because the contract resolves on one exact Binance noon ET minute close and current cushion is only about 3%.
quote_anchors: Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle close
market directionally right but somewhat overconfident
source_quality_view: Primary source quality is high for contract mechanics and current price context; contextual independence is medium; source-of-truth ambiguity is low to medium.
strongest_disconfirmers: No specific bearish catalyst emerged, and BTC is already above the threshold by about 3%.
If BTC stays rangebound or trends slightly up, Yes resolves comfortably.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT was around 74100 during the run, already above the 72000 threshold.
Independent contextual pricing also placed BTC in the mid-74k area on April 15.
Polymarket's adjacent strike ladder implies settlement expectations clustered only modestly above 72k.
timing_relevance: Very high: this is a single-minute noon ET settlement on April 17, so ordinary intraday movement matters more than in a daily-close market.
unresolved_ambiguities: Minor operational ambiguity remains between Binance UI presentation and API retrieval conventions, though not enough to dominate the case.
Current volatility evidence was qualitative rather than a full realized-vol dataset.
what_would_change_view: A materially larger cushion above 72k into April 16-17, or better evidence that a >3% downside move is unlikely in the remaining window, would move the estimate upward.
